notes: fan_mode 4 is Lenovo ideapad-laptop Efficient Thermal Dissipation Mode.
fan_mode 1 is Lenovo ideapad-laptop Standard Mode and is the balanced writable target.
fan_mode 0 is Lenovo ideapad-laptop Super Silent Mode and is the saver writable target.
fan_mode 16 was observed as a firmware-default readback value on this ThinkBook, but writing 16 returned Invalid argument on 2026-05-06. Do not use 16 as an auto-policy write target.
emergency preserves the selected platform profile and only applies fan_mode=4, so cooling does not rewrite the user's power-mode selection.
Do not read acpi_fan fan*_input on this BIOS; use platform_profile, VPC2004 fan_mode, hwmon temps, and thermal zones.
Post-BIOS RJCN31WW validation on 2026-05-07: sensors -j still returned fan1_input I/O errors and triggered ACPI VFAN._FST / _SB.FANL kernel errors, so RPM telemetry remains blocked.
Emergency hysteresis after RJCN31WW: if fan_mode is already 4, keep emergency until temperature is at or below recovery_temperature_c, not merely below hold_emergency_above_c.

Emergency minimum hold after RJCN31WW: keep fan_mode=4 for min_emergency_hold_seconds after a thermal-origin emergency or hold-threshold event, not after every repeated timer reconcile, to prevent both 4->1->4 oscillation and self-extending emergency hold.
Do not write PNP0C0B FAN0..FAN4 cooling_device cur_state on this BIOS; a controlled FAN0 write failed with missing ACPI method _SB.PC00.LPCB.UPFS.
Do not write VFAN cooling_device cur_state; it is tied to the broken VFAN/FANL telemetry path.

INTC1063 TFN1 exposes ACPI fan performance states and fine_grain_control=1. Guarded root writes of 50 to cooling_device1/cur_state succeeded on 2026-05-06 without new TFN1 kernel errors. One fan-validate run observed conservative max temperature moving from 105C to 98C over a short window, but cur_state/RPM feedback is still unavailable. Repeated fan-series runs on 2026-05-06 wrote level 50 successfully with kernel-ok, but possible cooling effect was only 1/3 in current_hot and 1/3 in current_warm; current_warm also saw a localized critical hotspot during the run. TFN1 remains manual/candidate-only.
Manual guarded route exists: abyss-machine cooling tfn1-write --level 50 --seconds 3 --json. This command is not used by auto-policy and refuses zero/off writes.

Validation route exists: abyss-machine cooling fan-validate --levels 50 --seconds 8 --interval 2 --json. It refuses zero/off writes and refuses lower-than-max TFN1 levels while hot/critical unless explicitly overridden.

Repeated validation route exists: abyss-machine cooling fan-series --level 50 --repeats 3 --seconds 8 --interval 2 --cooldown 5 --state-label current_hot --json. It is the required evidence route before any TFN1 automation discussion.

Thermal evidence route exists: abyss-machine cooling thermal-audit --seconds 10 --interval 2 --json. It records trusted sensor roles and keeps hwmon, firmware thermal-zone evidence, component sensors, and cpu_hotspot distribution separate.

abyss-machine 0.8.18+ includes temperature.summary.cpu_hotspot. Conservative max remains the fan/power safety signal, but CPU workload routing should avoid hot mapped cores instead of globally stopping all CPU use because one core is hot.
abyss-machine 0.8.14+ skips cur_state reads for TFN1 and for PNP0C0B FAN0..FAN4/VFAN so the auto timer does not repeatedly trigger the broken ACPI paths.

Thin-laptop policy corrected on 2026-05-12: 100-105C package/core temperature is a monitored active operating range for this machine when stable. Above 105C enters watch/routing territory; hard emergency gates are reserved for roughly 109-110C or firmware trip behavior.

abyss-machine 0.8.50 adds a central thermal episode classifier. Cooling status, thermal-audit, AI policy, CPU thermal-map, process thermal-plan, and resource planning now expose duration-aware episode evidence for instantaneous, seconds, brief, sustained, and long high-temperature runs.

Auto cooling now treats hot_temperature_c=106C and critical_temperature_c=109C. Emergency fan_mode=4 preserves the selected platform profile and does not kill, throttle, or re-affinitize running work.

RAPL smoothing enabled on 2026-05-12 and retuned on 2026-05-12: only in performance/ai, only after fan_mode=4 plus above-watch temperature and high package throttle delta across repeated samples. It temporarily caps RAPL-MMIO PL1 to 28W and restores 35W after recovery. It never changes TCC offset, BD PROCHOT, raw EC/PWM, or running process affinity.
